tool_args.label: Revenue Variance Drivers YTD October
tool_args.raw_text: Total revenue of $1,010.1 million was $48.5 million favorable to the forecast. This favorable variance was primarily due to higher other operating revenue and the timing of reimbursement for capital activity.

payload.claim_text: Total revenue of $1,010.1 million was $48.5 million favorable to the forecast. This favorable variance was primarily due to higher other operating revenue and the timing of reimbursement for capital activity.
payload.subject: LIRR

tool_args.label: Expense Variance Drivers YTD October
tool_args.raw_text: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $2,029.6 million were $5.7 million or 0.3% favorable to the forecast. The primary drivers of this favorable variance were due to lower payroll, associated fringe costs, and pensions. These favorable variances were partially offset by overtime, electric power, fuel, maintenance & other operating contracts, the timing of material and supplies, insurance, professional service contracts, and other business expenses.

payload.claim_text: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $2,029.6 million were $5.7 million or 0.3% favorable to the forecast. The primary drivers of this favorable variance were due to lower payroll, associated fringe costs, and pensions. These favorable variances were partially offset by overtime, electric power, fuel, maintenance & other operating contracts, the timing of material and supplies, insurance, professional service contracts, and other business expenses.
payload.subject: LIRR

tool_args.label: Preliminary Close Caveat
tool_args.raw_text: Results are based on the preliminary close of the general ledger and are subject to review and adjustment. Please note that the current months' actuals do not include post-close adjustments, which will be captured in the subsequent month's YTD results.

payload.gap_text: Results are based on the preliminary close of the general ledger and are subject to review and adjustment. Please note that the current months' actuals do not include post-close adjustments, which will be captured in the subsequent month's YTD results.
payload.description: The financial data is based on a preliminary close and may be adjusted in subsequent months
payload.missing_information: Post-close adjustments are not yet included

tool_args.label: Overtime Variance Drivers October
tool_args.raw_text: Total overtime was $8.3 million unfavorable. Non-reimbursable was $2.9 million favorable and reimbursable was $11.2 million unfavorable. Favorable non-reimbursable overtime was due to lower programmatic/routine maintenance, scheduled service, vacancy/absentee coverage, unscheduled service, and weather emergencies, partially offset by unscheduled maintenance.

payload.claim_text: Total overtime was $8.3 million unfavorable. Non-reimbursable was $2.9 million favorable and reimbursable was $11.2 million unfavorable. Favorable non-reimbursable overtime was due to lower programmatic/routine maintenance, scheduled service, vacancy/absentee coverage, unscheduled service, and weather emergencies, partially offset by unscheduled maintenance.
payload.subject: LIRR

tool_args.label: Labor Expense Variance Detail YTD October
tool_args.raw_text: Labor Expenses: $39.6 million favorable. Payroll expenses were $19.6 million favorable (lower average pay rates reflecting new hires)...

payload.claim_text: Labor Expenses: $39.6 million favorable. Payroll expenses were $19.6 million favorable (lower average pay rates reflecting new hires). Overtime expenses were $2.9 million favorable. Health & Welfare expenses were $11.2 million favorable (vacant positions). OPEB Current Payments were $0.3 million favorable (fewer retirees/beneficiaries). Pensions expenses were $3.2 million favorable due to the timing of pension payments, partially offset by percentage of pension allocated to reimbursable being over-estimated. Other Fringe Benefits expenses were $2.0 million unfavorable primarily due to higher FELA indemnity payments partially offset by lower Railroad Retirement Taxes. Reimbursable Overhead expenses were $4.4 million favorable primarily due to the timing of project activity.
payload.subject: LIRR

tool_args.label: Non-Labor Expense Variance Detail YTD October
tool_args.raw_text: Non-Labor Expenses: $1.5 million favorable. Electric Power expenses were $2.9 million unfavorable primarily due to higher rates...

payload.claim_text: Non-Labor Expenses: $1.5 million favorable. Electric Power expenses were $2.9 million unfavorable primarily due to higher rates, partially offset by lower consumption. Fuel expenses were $1.9 million unfavorable primarily due to higher diesel rates. Insurance expenses were $0.9 million unfavorable due to higher liability insurance, partially offset by lower property insurance. Claims expenses were $1.2 million favorable due to a decrease in reserves. Maintenance and Other Operating Contracts were $15.1 million favorable primarily due to higher project reimbursement for vehicles and work train usage, timing of the JCC building assessment, Moynihan train hall expenses, design and demolition plan of an existing building, OEM services and uniforms, partially offset by the timing of joint facility costs. Materials and Supplies were $5.9 million unfavorable primarily due to pooled materials charge out, miscellaneous inventory adjustment and right of way materials. Other Business Expenses were $3.3 million unfavorable primarily due to increase in bad debt reserves, partially offset by lower office supplies. Depreciation and Other were $7.0 million unfavorable (primarily depreciation and environmental remediation).
payload.subject: LIRR

tool_args.label: Adjusted Farebox Ratio Methodology
tool_args.raw_text: Adjusted Fare Box Operating Ratio and Cost Per Passenger indicators have been adjusted for comparability between the Long Island Rail Road and Metro-North Railroad...

payload.claim_text: Adjusted Farebox Operating Ratio and Cost Per Passenger indicators have been adjusted for comparability between the Long Island Rail Road and Metro-North Railroad and are being presented only at the railroad operating committees. Adjustments have been made to reflect all operating revenue and significant financial impacts that are outside management's control. These adjustments include: Inclusion of Other Operating Revenue, Removal of OPEB Current Payment expenses for retirees, and Removal of the UAAL associated with the LIRR's closed pension plan.
payload.subject: LIRR

tool_args.label: Standard Farebox Ratio Methodology
tool_args.raw_text: The Standard Farebox Operating Ratio and Cost Per Passenger indicators reflect MTA-wide adopted calculations that exclude non-cash liability adjustments: Depreciation, Other Post Employment Benefits and Environmental Remediation (GASB-49).

payload.claim_text: The Standard Farebox Operating Ratio and Cost Per Passenger indicators reflect MTA-wide adopted calculations that exclude non-cash liability adjustments: Depreciation, Other Post Employment Benefits and Environmental Remediation (GASB-49).
payload.subject: LIRR
